codeReview.summary: This fix adequately addresses the two remaining blockers from the prior failed review. `usePillars` now defers cache reads until after authenticated user resolution, and the pillar-selection state in ScriptGenerator, Ideas, and GenerateOutput now initializes safely and syncs after async pillar loading.

sharedStateObservations.observation: UI-verification procedure in `fullstack-worker` is still not being followed for UI-affecting component changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `fullstack-worker` requires `agent-browser` for UI verification (`.factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md:23,33,52`), but fix transcript skeleton for session `437514da-80bd-4558-b0ba-ad8052837b50` contains no `agent-browser` usage (`hasAgentBrowser: false`).

summary: Reviewed fix feature `fix-pillar-init-and-cache-scoping` against prior failure `fix-multi-user-scrutiny-blocking`. The new commit resolves both remaining blocking findings: async pillar initialization is now synchronized in all specified components, and user-scoped cache access in `usePillars` now occurs only after resolved current user ID.
